_comment: Curated offline cache for demo/package.json, keyed by package@version. References real published advisories (CVE/GHSA IDs) where they exist, used so grading/CI runs are deterministic and don't depend on live network access, rate limits, or a GITHUB_TOKEN. See README 'Offline demo fixtures' section.

colors@1.4.1.summary: No formal CVE was ever filed for this version. In January 2022 the maintainer intentionally sabotaged colors (and the related faker package), pushing an infinite-loop payload as a form of protest, which broke countless downstream builds. It's treated here purely as a behavioral anomaly (single maintainer, an erratic, out-of-pattern release) rather than a CVSS-scored vulnerability - there is no 'fixed version' to upgrade to, since it wasn't a code vulnerability with a patch; remediation is to pin to a pre-incident version (1.4.0) or replace the dependency.

minimist@1.2.0.summary: Prototype pollution in minimist allows an attacker to modify Object.prototype via a crafted __proto__ payload passed to the argument parser.

axios@1.5.0.summary: axios leaks the confidential XSRF-TOKEN cookie value in the Proxy-Authorization header when following a cross-origin redirect, allowing an attacker-controlled proxy target to obtain it.

moment@2.29.1.summary: moment's parsing of certain user-controlled date strings has inadequate complexity, allowing a regular-expression-denial-of-service (ReDoS) attack against applications that pass untrusted input to moment's parser.

xml2js@0.4.19.summary: Prototype pollution in xml2js allows attacker-controlled XML input to modify Object.prototype, potentially leading to denial of service or property injection in the host application.

node-ipc@9.2.1.summary: node-ipc versions 10.1.1 and 9.2.1 through 9.2.2 contained code that, based on the host machine's IP-derived geolocation, either overwrote or deleted arbitrary files on disk (the 2022 'protestware' incident). Treated here as a Critical-severity supply chain compromise, curated with a representative CVSS-equivalent score.
